title: Joins, Cardinality, Unmatched Rows, and Fanout Control · Integrity Triage
summary: Ticket, team, owner, and event data must be combined without losing unassigned work or multiplying effort totals. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a cardinality-safe support investigation and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Write explicit inner joins from relationship predicates and predict result cardinality before execution.
Use left, right, or full outer-join reasoning to retain required unmatched entities.
Model and query many-to-many relationships through an associative relation with its own grain.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.sql:
-- sql-basics cumulative relational data portfolio.
-- Each increment declares grain, predicts cardinality or changed rows, runs in fresh browser SQLite,
-- reconciles results, tests changed data, and records PostgreSQL or standard-SQL boundaries.

SELECT sqlite_version() AS browser_sqlite_version;

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Fanout Diagnosis
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable SQLite increment headed "-- Evidence: sql-fanout-diagnosis-1qzuml" with a concrete grain, hypothesis, or prediction; join relationship, unmatched-row, or fanout evidence; and an observation, reconciliation, or verification tied to returned rows, changed rows, an error, or a plan.

Evidence target: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Fanout Diagnosis.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Fanout Diagnosis into observable performance evidence. For Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Fanout Diagnosis, keep Fanout Diagnosis connected to executable relational evidence.

content.code:
-- Evidence: sql-fanout-diagnosis-1qzuml
-- grain: declare the exact row or mutation grain before running
SELECT teams.name, COUNT(tickets.ticket_id) AS ticket_count
FROM teams
LEFT JOIN tickets ON tickets.team_id = teams.team_id
GROUP BY teams.team_id, teams.name
ORDER BY teams.name;
-- observation: reconcile the returned rows, changed count, error, or plan with the prediction
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.
